campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and overall money management. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, including options for getting around by bus, bike, walking, and other methods beyond just cars.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and city finances
Monthly treasurer's financial report
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan for improving non-car transportation options

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could share their concerns about a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward by introducing the ordinance (the formal rule) and skipping the usual first reading step to speed up the process, with plans to finalize it at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for adoption
First reading of the ordinance waived to expedite approval
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to wrap up their 2026 weed removal program and establish new rules for how the council operates. They also discussed affordable housing policies, approved raising flags for Pride Month and Juneteenth celebrations, and reviewed transportation and housing plans with regional partners.
keyTopics: Completed weed abatement program for 2026
New official procedures and norms for City Council meetings
Affordable housing transfer rules
Pride and Juneteenth flag raisings approved
Regional transit and housing development planning

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council approved several contracts and agreements to move forward with construction projects and professional services. The council also voted to join a statewide insurance program that helps cities manage risk and reduce costs.
keyTopics: Construction contract approved with QLM, Inc.
New professional services agreement with Dudek (likely engineering/planning firm)
Joined California Joint Powers Insurance Authority for better insurance coverage
Multiple contract amendments and updates to existing agreements
Art transfer agreement authorization (details limited)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The City Council reviewed progress on programs helping San José's children and youth, including an alliance focused on youth empowerment and a summer safety initiative. They also heard updates on code enforcement activities and the library's plans to improve its buildings and services.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and programs to support young people in the city
Safe Summer Initiative Grant to keep kids safe during summer months
Code Enforcement Annual Report on city violations and enforcement actions
Library building improvements and customer experience upgrades

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that included both in-person and virtual attendance options. Residents could participate online via Zoom or phone to listen and provide public comments on agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom or phone line
Public comment procedures for remote participants
How to raise hand or unmute for speaking

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held their regular monthly meeting on April 9, 2026. The meeting included a flag salute, personal stories shared by three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale, and approval of the previous month's meeting notes from March 12.
keyTopics: Human Relations Commission monthly meeting
Personal stories shared by Commissioners 5, 6, and 7
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Community engagement and flag salute ceremony

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community initiatives including a presentation about Budget and Bites (likely a food/nutrition program), a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and information about summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved meeting minutes from the previous month and allowed residents time to speak about issues affecting the community.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
City of Cupertino summer jobs for young people
Approval of March 11 meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 where residents could participate either in person or remotely via Zoom. The city is making it easy for the public to stay involved by offering multiple ways to join and submit comments about agenda items.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format allows in-person and remote participation
Zoom access available with meeting ID and phone dial-in option
Written public comments can be submitted via eComment on the City Council Agenda page

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve several administrative and financial matters, including joining a tax board partnership with the county, considering fee waivers for a community educational organization, and moving forward with construction project contracts. The council also reviewed updates to the city's housing and community development plans and considered new requests from individual council members.
keyTopics: Tax Board Partnership - City will participate in Franchise Tax Board program with county
Fee Waivers - Milpitas Community Educational organization requested fee breaks and donations
Construction Contracts - City approved project plans and awarded contracts for construction work
Housing & Development Plans - Updates to 2025-2030 community development strategy discussed
Council Member Requests - Review of new workplan items proposed by individual councilmembers

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss the city's natural gas utility plans and finances for the next fiscal year. The main decision involved approving changes to how much residents and businesses pay for gas service, with different rate adjustments for residential customers, apartment buildings, and large commercial users.
keyTopics: Natural gas rate increases for residential and commercial customers
Fiscal Year 2027 gas utility budget and financial forecast
Reserve fund transfers and general fund adjustments
Rate schedule changes for three customer categories (G-1, G-2, G-3)
